rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLEN YOSHIO YASAKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/28/2020. The following issues must be resolved: 1) Has any heir or beneficiary "previously been" confined in any correctional facility identified in Probate Code § 9202(b)? 2) The date notice was given to the Franchise Tax Board must be identified. The manner of giving notice must also be identified. See LRSF 14.67(L)(2). 3) The decedent's mother's creditor claim was filed after the claim filing period and more than one year after the decedent's death. Why should the claim be allowed? 4) Did the petitioner give notice of proposed action before donating the Honda Civic? See Probate Code § 10513. 5) How did the petitioner acquire more shares of each security than the number of shares listed on the inventory and appraisal? The cost basis of additional shares of each security must also be identified for purposes of calculating gains or losses. 6) How did the petitioner determine each "net value" of fractional interests in the LLCs for purposes of proposing nonprorata distributions? *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORES ANN JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/28/2020. The following issues must be resolved: 1) Distributions from other estates and trusts must be appraised by a probate referee. A corrected inventory and appraisal including Attachment 2 must be filed. 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The proposed distribution must specify each distributee's share of unknown or newly discovered property of the estate. B) Paragraph 5 of the prayer for relief must identify specific property to be distributed as well as names and shares of distributees. The general descriptions "to the persons entitled to it" and "as set forth in the petition" are insufficient. *No courtesy copy of the subject petition has been received by the assigned examiner as of 07/01/2020. **Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OLLIE MAE MIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/21/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proof of publication is on file. 2) No declaration of real property is on file. See LRSF 14.27C. 3) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The newspaper must be specified in item 1 of the petition. B) Item 5(2)(a) or (b) of the petition must be checked. C) Item 8 of the petition must specify the relationship of each person to the decedent. D) The proof of service of notice of petition to administer estate shows that the server's address is in Oakland. Why is the place of mailing in El Sobrante? *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols.
